sutta: Monks, I know not of any other single thing that conduces to the confusion to the disappearance of true dharma as does negligence negligence indeed conduces to the confusion and disappearance of true dharma then.\n\nThe Buddha said monks those monks who point out what is not dharma as Dhamma such conduct of theirs is to the loss of many folk to the misery of many folk to the loss the injury the misery of devas and mankind moreover such beget great demerit and caused the disappearance of this true dharma uh that's that's the suta.
commentary: These two suttas also tell us that nHere the Buddha is saying that there are some monks who do not understand the dharma enough so that what is not dharma a Dhamma what is contradictory to the Buddha's teachings they say it is the Buddha's teachings and because of that now they create they create great the merit and they cause the disappearance of the true dharma now that's why it is very important for monks as to study the teachings of the Buddha which the Buddha calls in the suttas as Dhamma Vinaya as I pointed out before the Buddha never taught the tripitaka the Buddha taught the Dhamma Vinaya as he had said in the suttas the word tipitaka or tripitaka was never spoken by the Buddha the Buddha only said Dhamma Vinaya was taught by him and the Dhamma in the suttas in the anguttara nikaya book of the force chapter of the force the Buddha says that Dhamma is the suttas are the Buddha's teachings so because some monks are not very familiar with the suttas what is not dharma what is contradictory to the Dhamma they say is Dhamma and so because of that they cause they help to cause the disappearance of this true dharma one point eleven point one the Buddha said those monks who point out what is not dharma as not dharma such conduct of theirs is to the prophet of many folk to the happiness of many folk to the good prophet and happiness of devas and mankind moreover such monks beget great merit and establish this true dharma now this sutta here the Buddha is saying that among I should point out now what is not dharma as not dharma because in that case they will profit many many people and also they would help to preserve this true Dhamma that is why if we know that something that is being taught now is not according to the Dhamma we should speak out to preserve the true dharma this is not being controversial but just following the advice of the Buddha in fact the Buddha also during his time used to speak out against a lot of wrong teachings that were prevalent during his time and because of that he was also reviled by a lot of these external teachers and in one sutta he said I do not quarrel with the world he said the world quarrels with me one who teaches the Dhamma the true dharma does not quarrel with the world so here we would be following the Buddha's footsteps if we try to preserve the true dharma by speaking out against what is not dharma then.
